turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having some problems with my stomach. I'm getting really bad diarrhea, like 8-10 times a day, and it's been going on for about two weeks now.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The crampy lower abdominal pain, especially on my left side, is pretty constant. It feels like I have a knot there that just won't go away.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about two weeks ago, but I'd say it really kicked into high gear about 5-6 days ago. The diarrhea and cramping just got much worse around then.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens every time I go to the bathroom, which is usually like 8-10 times a day. Sometimes it's more urgent than others, but it's always loose or watery.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's an 8 out of 10 in terms of severity - it's really impacting my daily life and work, and I'm getting frustrated with not being able to find any relief. The cramping and urgency are making me feel anxious and worried about what might be causing this.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: Yes, I get really tired afterwards, like my whole body feels drained. It's affecting my work performance too - I'm a software engineer at a startup and we're in crunch time right now, but I'm having trouble keeping up with everything because of this constant fatigue and urgency to use the bathroom.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: It definitely gets worse after eating, especially if it's something greasy or spicy. And of course, it gets a lot worse when I'm working long hours at my desk - the stress doesn't help, and I end up having to get up from my computer multiple times an hour because of the urgency.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: No, I don't experience any of those symptoms. To be honest, I'm more worried about the diarrhea and cramping than anything else - it's just so constant and frustrating that I haven't really noticed anything else besides feeling really tired afterwards.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: No, it's mostly confined to my lower abdomen, left side. Sometimes it feels like it radiates up a little bit towards my back, but that's about it.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any swelling in my legs or feet. To be honest, I'm more worried about getting to the bottom of this diarrhea issue and finding some relief from it - that's been taking over my life lately.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've actually been taking loperamide, Imodium, for the past couple of weeks in an attempt to manage the diarrhea. It hasn't really helped much, so I'm hoping we can figure out what's going on and get me some real relief.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: I don't think I have anything like that. My dad has type 2 diabetes, but my mom is healthy as far as I know.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I'm not sure about that. To be honest, I don't think I've ever had my cholesterol checked or any blood work done recently. It's been over 2 years since I last saw a doctor, and everything has just kind of fallen through the cracks with work being so busy lately.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I do drink beer, but not excessively, usually 2-3 beers most weeknights. Lately, though, I've been having a few more than that due to stress from work and these symptoms, sometimes up to 4-5 per night.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: I'd say my diet is pretty bad, to be honest. I've been working long hours and mostly just grabbing takeout or eating at restaurants because it's easier than cooking for myself. I don't really have a regular meal schedule anymore, which I'm sure isn't helping things.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: To be honest, my dad has type 2 diabetes, but I don't think he's had any issues with his heart. He's actually pretty healthy for a guy in his 60s. But my older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, so there might be some underlying issues that run in our family, I'm not sure.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: Actually, my mom is pretty healthy and doesn't have any major medical conditions. My dad has type 2 diabetes like you mentioned, but that's about it. And my sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, which makes me a bit worried, to be honest. I'm not sure if there might be something genetic going on here.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: It's been over two years since I last saw a doctor. To be honest, I've been putting it off because of work and just haven't had the time or energy to deal with getting checked out.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. I'm not aware of any medication allergies that I've ever experienced. Like I said earlier, I've been taking loperamide for the past couple of weeks with minimal relief, but if there's something else we can try to get this under control, I'm all for it at this point.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: Honestly, I've been worried about getting to the bottom of this diarrhea issue because my older sister had Crohn's disease diagnosed at a pretty young age. It makes me wonder if there might be something similar going on here. And like I mentioned earlier, I've also noticed some unintentional weight loss over the past few weeks - about 4 kg (9 lbs) in total.
